title: Joint Call of European & Global Industry for the Removal of Article 27a of the Proposed Revision of the CBAM Regulation
content: As of 1st January 2026, the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) has entered into force with full compliance obligations. Within a week, a substantial change was proposed, undermining a framework that took years to agree on and implement: Article 27a, which introduces an openâ€‘ended, possibly retroactive suspension mechanism for some products in the CBAM list. While this article may seem like a potential opportunity for the sectors concerned by the suspension, it will actually cause uncertainty, unpredictability and a potential loss of billions of investments for sectors such as low-carbon ammonia, steel and fertilisers â€“ especially due to its possible retroactivity. Hydrogen Europe and 5 other organisations representing clean hydrogen, ammonia, fertilisers, have sent a joint letter to the European Parliamentâ€™s ENVI committee calling for the removal of such article from the CBAM revision.
author: Analysts Account
